task_text: Look at the sprint 14 assignments and testing log, then tell me which tasks are at risk of missing the sprint deadline. Write your assessment to sprint14_deadline_risk.txt.

content.text: I’ll review the sprint 14 assignments and testing log to identify at-risk tasks and draft the deadline risk report.

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

content.text: I’ve identified the tasks most likely to miss the sprint deadline. Now I’ll compile the full risk assessment into your requested file.

input.content:
Sprint 14 Deadline Risk Assessment
Sprint Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18
Analysis Date: 2026-07-11 (based on testing log cut)
Source Files: sprint_14_task_assignments.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt

=== EXECUTIVE SUMMARY ===
Based on task status as of 07-11 and QA results (10/15 executed, 2 FAIL, 3 NOT RUN), 5 tasks are at elevated risk of missing the 07-18 sprint deadline, with 2 critical HIGH severity defects blocking the data pipeline.

Overall Sprint Health: AT RISK

=== TASKS AT RISK (Ranked by Severity) ===

1. HEL-243 - Data ingestion: date format normalization [CRITICAL RISK]
   Assignee: Amir Hassan | Points: 5 | Due: 2026-07-15 | Status: Blocked
   
   Evidence:
   - Status is explicitly Blocked in assignments
   - QA Defect HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format - FAIL (TC-505)
   - Severity: HIGH - Affects ~40% of Meridian source data
   - Blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15 (same day as task due date)
   - This leaves zero buffer to implement/fix and re-test on due date
   
   Impact: Blocks HEL-245 data pipeline integration tests. Meridian is a key client deliverable.
   Likelihood of missing deadline: VERY HIGH

2. HEL-246 - UAT environment: server provisioning [CRITICAL RISK]
   Assignee: Nadia Petrov | Points: 8 | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: At Risk
   
   Evidence:
   - Explicitly flagged as "At Risk" in assignment sheet
   - Largest point value in sprint (8 points) - suggests complexity
   - Due 2026-07-16, only 5 days from current log date, still not Done
   - UAT environment is typically a dependency for final validation and Performance benchmark (HEL-247)
   
   Impact: Could cascade and block HEL-245, HEL-247, and overall UAT readiness.
   Likelihood of missing deadline: HIGH

3. HEL-245 - Integration test suite: data pipeline [HIGH RISK]
   Assignee: Kenji Watanabe | Points: 5 | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: In Progress
   
   Evidence:
   - Test Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL - TC-505 and TC-507
   - Dependent on HEL-D-014 (blocked) and HEL-D-015 (memory overflow at ~210k records, root cause under investigation)
   - Both defects Severity: HIGH, Owner: Amir Hassan
   - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion (>200k) causes pipeline crash - no workaround identified yet
   - End of sprint due date, but depends on HEL-243 being unblocked first
   
   Impact: Core data pipeline delivery at risk. 2 open HIGH defects with 7 days left.
   Likelihood of missing deadline: HIGH

4. HEL-247 - Performance benchmark: dashboard load [MEDIUM-HIGH RISK]
   Assignee: Derek Okafor | Points: 3 | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: Not Started
   
   Evidence:
   - Status Not Started as of 07-11, with only 7 days left in sprint
   - QA Test Case TC-604 Load time benchmark (100k records) = NOT RUN
   - Depends on HEL-242 (chart rendering) completion and likely HEL-246 (UAT provisioning)
   - If UAT env not ready, benchmark cannot run
   
   Impact: Q2 deliverable may be at risk if performance cannot be verified.
   Likelihood of missing deadline: MEDIUM-HIGH

5. HEL-242 - Dashboard: chart rendering module [MEDIUM RISK]
   Assignee: Tomas Rivera | Points: 8 | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: In Progress
   
   Evidence:
   - TC-602 Chart rendering - line chart = IN PROGRESS, TC-603 bar chart = NOT RUN
   - 8-point task, In Progress 4 days before due date (as of 07-11 assessment)
   - Large scope, dashboard suite only 1/4 complete
   
   Impact: Blocks HEL-247 performance testing and dashboard deliverable.
   Likelihood of missing deadline: MEDIUM

=== TASKS LOWER RISK ===

HEL-241 - Dashboard: data table component - In Progress, due 2026-07-14
  - TC-601 PASS, appears on track despite near due date. Monitor closely.

HEL-244 - Integration test suite: auth flows - Done, 4/4 PASS - No risk

HEL-248 - GeoSync license renewal coordination - In Progress, due 2026-07-14
  - 2 points, administrative. Due imminently but low technical risk unless external vendor delay.

=== DEPENDENCY CHAIN ANALYSIS ===
Meridian sample data (external, due 07-15) -> HEL-243 (Blocked) -> HEL-D-014 fix -> HEL-245 (data pipeline tests)
HEL-D-015 (memory overflow, RCA pending) -> HEL-245 -> Sprint completion
HEL-246 (UAT provisioning, At Risk) -> HEL-247 (Performance benchmark)
HEL-242 (chart module) -> HEL-247 + Dashboard TC-603/TC-604

Critical Path Bottleneck: Amir Hassan owns both HIGH severity defects (HEL-D-014, HEL-D-015) affecting HEL-243. Single-person dependency.

=== RECOMMENDATIONS ===
1. Escalate HEL-243: Contact Meridian for early sample data or use synthetic MM/DD/YYYY data to unblock development before 07-15.
2. Pair program/support for HEL-D-015 memory overflow - assign additional engineer to Amir as defect is investigating.
3. Daily check-in on HEL-246 with Nadia Petrov + infra team - identify provisioning blocker (budget, capacity, access).
4. Consider descoping HEL-247 performance benchmark to 10k records baseline if 100k blocked, or moving to Sprint 15 with stakeholder approval.
5. Review Tomas Rivera workload for HEL-242 - may need QA support to run TC-602/TC-603 in parallel.

=== CONCLUSION ===
3 tasks at critical/high risk of missing deadline (HEL-243, HEL-246, HEL-245), 2 at medium risk (HEL-247, HEL-242). Sprint 14 deadline of 2026-07-18 is at risk unless blockers are resolved by 2026-07-15.

content.text:
Assessment complete and written to **sprint14_deadline_risk.txt**.

**Summary of Tasks at Risk of Missing Sprint 14 Deadline (Ends 2026-07-18):**

**CRITICAL RISK:**
- **HEL-243** Data ingestion: date format normalization (Amir Hassan) - **Blocked**
  - Blocked by Defect HEL-D-014: MM/DD/YYYY normalization FAIL (TC-505)
  - Affects 40% of Meridian data, HIGH severity
  - Blocked pending external sample data due *same day* as task due date (2026-07-15) - zero buffer
  - Very high likelihood of miss

- **HEL-246** UAT environment: server provisioning (Nadia Petrov) - **At Risk** flag
  - 8-point task, largest in sprint, still not Done 5 days before due date (2026-07-16)
  - Cascading dependency for performance testing and UAT

**HIGH RISK:**
- **HEL-245** Integration test suite: data pipeline (Kenji Watanabe) - Due 2026-07-18
  - 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL: TC-505 (HEL-D-014) and TC-507 (HEL-D-015 memory overflow at ~210k records)
  - Both HIGH severity defects owned by Amir Hassan, RCA still pending on HEL-D-015
  - Depends on HEL-243 unblocking

**MEDIUM-HIGH RISK:**
- **HEL-247** Performance benchmark: dashboard load (Derek Okafor) - **Not Started** as of 07-11, due 07-18
  - QA case TC-604 NOT RUN, depends on HEL-246 and HEL-242

**MEDIUM RISK:**
- **HEL-242** Dashboard: chart rendering module (Tomas Rivera) - 8 points, In Progress, due 07-16
  - TC-602 IN PROGRESS, TC-603 NOT RUN, Dashboard suite only 1/4 complete

**On Track:** HEL-244 Done, HEL-241 likely okay (TC-601 PASS), HEL-248 low tech risk.

Critical bottleneck: External Meridian data dependency + single-owner (Amir Hassan) for 2 HIGH defects blocking the pipeline critical path.
